name: Document Evidence — 50Q
description: 50 document QA questions requiring retrieval and synthesis from contracts, audit reports, policy documents, and close memos.

cases.name: Acme MSA Payment Terms
cases.input: What are the payment terms in the Acme Software MSA?
cases.expectedOutput: Per the Acme MSA, payment is due Net-30 from invoice date. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month.

cases.name: Acme MSA Renewal Clause
cases.input: Does the Acme MSA auto-renew?
cases.expectedOutput: The Acme MSA auto-renews annually unless either party provides 60 days written notice of non-renewal prior to the renewal date.

cases.name: Audit Material Weaknesses
cases.input: What material weaknesses were identified in the FY2025 external audit?
cases.expectedOutput: No material weaknesses were identified. One significant deficiency in revenue recognition timing controls was noted and management committed to remediation.

cases.name: ASC 606 Five-Step Model
cases.input: What are the five steps of the ASC 606 revenue recognition model?
cases.expectedOutput: Step 1: Identify the contract. Step 2: Identify performance obligations. Step 3: Determine transaction price. Step 4: Allocate price to obligations. Step 5: Recognize revenue when obligations satisfied.

cases.name: Close Memo Q3 Adjustments
cases.input: What adjustments were made in the Q3 FY2025 close memo?
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 close memo includes 4 adjustments: deferred revenue reclassification $1.2M, software capitalization adjustment $0.4M, accrued bonus true-up $0.3M, AR reserve increase $0.2M.

cases.name: SOX Key Controls
cases.input: What are the key SOX controls over financial reporting?
cases.expectedOutput: Key SOX controls: period-end close process, revenue recognition review, access controls, segregation of duties, and management review controls over estimates.

cases.name: Acme Contract Value
cases.input: What is the total contract value of the Acme Software agreement?
cases.expectedOutput: The Acme Software MSA has a total contract value of $3.6M over a 3-year term ($1.2M ACV).

cases.name: Audit Scope
cases.input: What was the scope of the FY2025 external audit?
cases.expectedOutput: The external audit covered the consolidated financial statements for FY2025, including all subsidiaries with revenues exceeding $5M.

cases.name: Retention Policy Duration
cases.input: How long must financial records be retained per policy?
cases.expectedOutput: Financial records must be retained for a minimum of 7 years per SOX and SEC requirements. AI-generated drafts are retained for 3 years.

cases.name: Close Sign-Off Status
cases.input: Was the Q3 FY2025 close formally signed off?
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 FY2025 close was signed off by the Controller on October 15, 2025, and CFO on October 16, 2025. All checklists marked complete.

cases.name: Acme SLA Terms
cases.input: What are the SLA commitments in the Acme MSA?
cases.expectedOutput: The Acme MSA guarantees 99.9% uptime. For downtime exceeding SLA, Acme is entitled to service credits of 10% of monthly fee per hour of excess downtime, capped at 30% of monthly fee.

cases.name: Acme Termination Clause
cases.input: Under what conditions can the Acme MSA be terminated for cause?
cases.expectedOutput: Termination for cause requires written notice and a 30-day cure period. Material breach, insolvency, or failure to meet SLAs for 3 consecutive months are grounds for cause termination.

cases.name: Revenue Recognition Gap
cases.input: What revenue recognition gap was identified in the audit?
cases.expectedOutput: The significant deficiency noted in the audit relates to manual revenue recognition controls for multi-element arrangements. Specifically, the SSP documentation process lacks systematic review. Management's remediation plan includes automated SSP workflow by Q2 FY2026.

cases.name: Variable Consideration Policy
cases.input: How does our policy treat variable consideration under ASC 606?
cases.expectedOutput: Variable consideration is estimated using the expected value or most-likely-amount method. It is included in transaction price only to the extent it is probable no significant revenue reversal will occur. Constraints are applied to milestone-based and usage-based contracts.

cases.name: Q3 Intercompany Reconciliation
cases.input: Were there any intercompany reconciliation issues in the Q3 close?
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 close memo notes one intercompany timing difference of $0.3M between the US and UK entities due to a delayed interco invoice. Resolved post-close with a Q4 true-up entry approved by the Controller.

cases.name: Acme Data Processing Terms
cases.input: What data processing obligations does Acme have under our MSA?
cases.expectedOutput: Per the DPA addendum to the Acme MSA, Acme is a data processor. Data may only be processed for specified purposes, must be encrypted in transit and at rest, and Acme must notify us of any breach within 72 hours.

cases.name: Audit Opinion Type
cases.input: What type of opinion did the external auditor issue for FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: The external auditor issued an unmodified (clean) opinion on the FY2025 consolidated financial statements. The opinion noted the significant deficiency but did not qualify the opinion.

cases.name: SSP Documentation Requirements
cases.input: What are the SSP documentation requirements in our revenue recognition policy?
cases.expectedOutput: SSP must be documented for each distinct performance obligation where VSOE is not available. Acceptable methods include adjusted market assessment, expected cost plus margin, and residual approach. Documentation must be reviewed and approved by the Controller annually.

cases.name: Q3 Software Capitalization
cases.input: How much software was capitalized in Q3 FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: $0.9M of internal-use software development costs were capitalized in Q3 per the close memo. These are in the application development stage of projects meeting ASC 350-40 criteria.

cases.name: Disclosure Controls Assessment
cases.input: What is the assessment of disclosure controls effectiveness?
cases.expectedOutput: Management assessed disclosure controls as effective as of the annual assessment date. The significant deficiency in revenue recognition controls does not rise to material weakness level and does not affect the overall conclusion.

cases.name: Multi-Document Revenue Recognition
cases.input: Does the Acme MSA meet the ASC 606 five-step criteria for revenue recognition?
cases.expectedOutput: Step 1: Contract identified — MSA + SOW qualifies. Step 2: Two POs identified: SaaS license + implementation services. Step 3: Transaction price $3.6M. Step 4: SSP allocation needed — documented per policy. Step 5: SaaS recognized ratably; implementation on percentage-of-completion. Compliance: in-line with policy except SSP documentation needs update for implementation services SSP.

cases.name: Audit Finding Remediation
cases.input: What is the status of remediation for the audit significant deficiency?
cases.expectedOutput: Remediation per management's plan: (1) Automated SSP workflow to be implemented by Q2 FY2026. (2) Quarterly SSP documentation review added to close checklist (implemented Q4 FY2025). (3) Training for revenue team on new documentation requirements (completed October 2025). External auditor will re-assess in Q2 FY2026.

cases.name: Contract Risk Assessment
cases.input: Identify the top 3 contractual risk areas in the Acme MSA.
cases.expectedOutput: (1) SLA exposure: uncapped uptime credits above 30% threshold could exceed $30K/month at $1.2M ACV. (2) Data processing: GDPR notification obligations require 72hr breach response — test incident response plan. (3) IP ownership: custom development work-for-hire clause needs review — ambiguous scope for AI-generated outputs.

cases.name: SOX Material Weakness Threshold
cases.input: At what point would the revenue recognition deficiency escalate to a material weakness?
cases.expectedOutput: Under AS 2201, a significant deficiency becomes a material weakness if: (1) misstatement exceeds 5% of revenue ($18.7M) or $10M threshold; (2) management cannot demonstrate compensating controls; (3) deficiency exists in high-risk control areas with no effective compensating control. Current deficiency is below threshold with compensating manual controls in place.

cases.name: Close Process Completeness
cases.input: Was the Q3 close process complete and compliant with policy?
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 close was complete and compliant: all 28 checklist items signed off, 4 adjusting entries approved per policy, interco reconciliation resolved post-close with proper documentation. One exception: bank reconciliation approved 3 days past the 5-day close target — noted as process improvement item.

cases.name: IFRS 15 vs ASC 606
cases.input: What are the key differences between IFRS 15 and ASC 606 for our contracts?
cases.expectedOutput: Core models are largely converged. Key differences: (1) IFRS 15 uses a single set of standards globally; ASC 606 has SEC staff guidance for public companies. (2) License revenue timing can differ for IP licenses (point-in-time vs ratable). (3) Practical expedients differ slightly. For our contracts, the most impactful difference is in multi-year SaaS with variable pricing under IFRS 15 — requires constraint analysis at each reporting period.

cases.name: Contract Modification Policy
cases.input: How does our policy handle contract modifications under ASC 606?
cases.expectedOutput: Contract modifications are treated as: (1) Separate contract — if distinct goods/services at standalone price. (2) Prospective modification — if remaining goods/services are distinct but not at standalone price (common). (3) Cumulative catch-up — if remaining goods/services are not distinct from original contract. Most SaaS contract modifications (seat changes, upsells) are treated as prospective.

cases.name: Audit Planning Materiality
cases.input: What materiality threshold was used by the external auditors?
cases.expectedOutput: External auditors used a planning materiality of $4.7M (5% of pre-tax income) and a performance materiality of $3.3M (70% of planning materiality). The significant deficiency was identified at $0.8M, below performance materiality.

cases.name: Accounts Receivable Reserve Policy
cases.input: How does our policy determine the AR reserve?
cases.expectedOutput: AR reserve is determined using an aging analysis approach: 0-30 days 0%, 31-60 days 5%, 61-90 days 15%, 91-120 days 40%, >120 days 75%, and specific reserves for identified doubtful accounts. Reserve is reviewed and approved quarterly by the Controller.

cases.name: Implementation Services Revenue
cases.input: How is implementation services revenue recognized?
cases.expectedOutput: Implementation services revenue is recognized using the input method (labor hours incurred / total estimated hours). If implementation is bundled with SaaS, SSP allocation is required. Fixed-fee implementations use percentage-of-completion. Time-and-material implementations recognize as services are performed.

cases.name: Internal Audit Coverage
cases.input: What areas were covered by internal audit in FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: FY2025 internal audit covered: revenue recognition controls (Q1), procure-to-pay (Q2), payroll and equity compensation (Q3), IT general controls and access management (Q4). All audits resulted in low or medium findings. No high or critical findings were issued.

cases.name: Capitalized Software Policy
cases.input: When can software development costs be capitalized?
cases.expectedOutput: Under ASC 350-40 (internal-use software), costs are capitalized during the application development stage only. Preliminary project stage and post-implementation maintenance costs are expensed. Capitalization begins when technological feasibility is established and ceases when the software is ready for use.

cases.name: Acme Contract Governing Law
cases.input: What law governs the Acme Software MSA?
cases.expectedOutput: The Acme MSA is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware. Disputes are to be resolved by binding arbitration in New York, NY.

cases.name: Acme Limitation of Liability
cases.input: What is the liability cap in the Acme MSA?
cases.expectedOutput: Liability is capped at the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. This applies to both parties except in cases of gross negligence, willful misconduct, or IP indemnification, which are uncapped.

cases.name: Earnings Release Disclosure
cases.input: What non-GAAP metrics are required to be reconciled in earnings releases?
cases.expectedOutput: Per SEC guidance and our disclosure policy, non-GAAP metrics (Adjusted EBITDA, Non-GAAP EPS, Free Cash Flow) must include a reconciliation to the most directly comparable GAAP measure. ARR and NRR are operating metrics and require definitional disclosure but no GAAP reconciliation.

cases.name: Going Concern Assessment
cases.input: Is there any going concern risk noted in the audit or close documentation?
cases.expectedOutput: No going concern indicators were identified. The company is FCF positive, has $142M cash, and no debt covenants at risk. The auditors evaluated going concern per ASC 205-40 and found no substantial doubt about the entity's ability to continue as a going concern for the next 12 months.

cases.name: Related Party Transactions
cases.input: Were any related party transactions disclosed in FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: One related party transaction was disclosed: a cloud services agreement with a vendor where a board member holds a 4% equity stake. Transaction value $0.4M, priced at arm's-length market rates. Approved by the Audit Committee.

cases.name: Goodwill Impairment
cases.input: Has goodwill been tested for impairment and what were the results?
cases.expectedOutput: Annual goodwill impairment test performed as of October 31, 2025. One reporting unit with $14.2M goodwill (acquired FY2022). Qualitative assessment concluded no impairment triggers. Fair value of reporting unit significantly exceeds carrying value. No quantitative test required.

cases.name: Multi-Year Contract Revenue Profile
cases.input: How is revenue from a 3-year SaaS contract with annual price escalations recognized?
cases.expectedOutput: Under ASC 606, variable consideration from annual price escalations (if contractually defined, e.g., 5% annual increase) is estimated and allocated to each performance period. If escalations are substantive, revenue may not equal cash billings in any given year. The 'consistent allocation' practical expedient may be applied if escalations reflect standalone prices at each year.

cases.name: Contract Costs Capitalization
cases.input: How are sales commission costs capitalized under ASC 340-40?
cases.expectedOutput: Sales commissions that are incremental costs of obtaining a contract are capitalized as contract assets under ASC 340-40 if the amortization period exceeds one year. Amortized on a straight-line basis over the expected period of benefit (typically contract term + expected renewal period). We use a 3-year average benefit period for enterprise contracts.

cases.name: Acme IP Ownership
cases.input: Who owns intellectual property created during the Acme implementation?
cases.expectedOutput: Per the Acme MSA IP clause: (1) Our platform IP remains ours. (2) Custom configurations built by our professional services team are work-for-hire owned by Acme. (3) General platform improvements inspired by but not specific to Acme remain our property. (4) Data generated by Acme in the platform remains Acme's property.

cases.name: Hedge Accounting Policy
cases.input: Does the company use hedge accounting for FX exposures?
cases.expectedOutput: Per the disclosure policy, the company does not currently designate hedges for hedge accounting treatment under ASC 815. FX derivatives (if used) are marked to market through earnings. Natural hedges via local-currency billing are the primary FX management tool.

cases.name: Equity Compensation Expense
cases.input: How is stock-based compensation expense recognized?
cases.expectedOutput: Stock-based compensation is recognized using the grant-date fair value method under ASC 718. Options and RSUs are expensed on a straight-line basis over the vesting period. Forfeiture estimates are updated each period. ESPP discount is expensed as compensation.

cases.name: Comparative Period Adjustments
cases.input: Were any prior period adjustments made in FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: One prior period error correction was identified and recorded in Q2 FY2025: a $0.4M overstatement of PS revenue in Q4 FY2024 due to a milestone recognized prematurely. Restatement was immaterial; recorded as a cumulative adjustment to opening retained earnings per ASC 250.

cases.name: Commitments and Contingencies
cases.input: What significant commitments and contingencies are disclosed?
cases.expectedOutput: Disclosed commitments: office leases $8.2M total obligation through FY2028, cloud infrastructure minimum commitments $12.4M. Contingencies: two pending customer disputes totaling $1.4M potential liability — management assesses as probable resolution with no payment due, consistent with prior periods.

cases.name: Tax Rate Analysis
cases.input: What is our effective tax rate and why does it differ from the statutory rate?
cases.expectedOutput: Effective tax rate for FY2025 is 22.4% vs 21% federal statutory rate. Reconciling items: state taxes +2.1%, R&D credits -2.8%, non-deductible SBC +1.8%, excess tax benefits from equity awards -1.7%, FDII deduction -0.8%, other +0.8%.

cases.name: Concentration of Credit Risk
cases.input: What concentration of credit risk is disclosed?
cases.expectedOutput: Concentration risk: 72% of AR from North America. Top 10 customers represent 28% of AR balance. No single customer exceeds 5% of AR. Credit quality is assessed as high (97% investment-grade customers by revenue). Historical bad debt rate is 0.3% of revenue.

cases.name: Subsequent Events
cases.input: Were any subsequent events disclosed after FY2025 year-end?
cases.expectedOutput: Two subsequent events disclosed: (1) Signed enterprise contract Jan 8 ($1.8M ACV) — this is the slipped Q4 deal. (2) Announced restructuring plan Jan 15 affecting 4% of workforce, estimated $2.4M one-time charge in Q1 FY2026.

cases.name: Performance Obligation Identification
cases.input: How many performance obligations exist in a typical SaaS-plus-implementation contract?
cases.expectedOutput: Typical bundled contract has 2-3 POs: (1) SaaS subscription license (satisfied ratably over contract term), (2) Implementation/onboarding services (satisfied over project period), (3) Optional: Premium support package (satisfied ratably). Each PO must be distinct — capable of being distinct in context of the contract and capable of being distinct on its own.

cases.name: Multi-Doc Synthesis: Compliance Status
cases.input: Based on the audit report, close memo, and ASC 606 policy, provide an overall assessment of revenue recognition compliance for Q3 FY2025.
cases.expectedOutput: Overall compliance is satisfactory with one identified exception. Audit: significant deficiency in SSP documentation for multi-element arrangements (not material weakness). Close memo Q3: $1.2M deferred revenue reclassification was appropriate per policy; software capitalization $0.9M correctly capitalized per ASC 350-40. Policy: SSP documentation gap is being remediated per plan. Net assessment: revenue is materially correct, controls deficiency is known and being addressed, no audit qualification.
